title: McMahon’s 2026 Onondaga County budget includes tax rate cut, new police drones, helicopter
summary: Onondaga County Executive Ryan McMahon's proposed 2026 budget totals $1.6 billion with a 3% spending increase while maintaining flat property tax levy at $146 million. Key initiatives include $6 million for a new sheriff's helicopter, $700,000 for a drone-as-first-responder emergency program, $300,000 for body-worn cameras for custody deputies, and continued funding for lead remediation ($1.23 million), early childhood services ($2.3 million), and school mental health programs.

political_signal.description: County executive is signaling fiscal discipline (flat property tax levy despite 3% spending increase) while prioritizing modernization of public safety infrastructure (helicopter replacement, drone program) and continued investment in preventive health/social services (lead remediation, mental health, early childhood).

processing_notes: Dollar amounts extracted include both aggregates ($1.6B budget) and line items; sewer charge correction noted in article (update from $24/$478 to $25/$478.58)
City of Syracuse cross-reference: parallel drone initiative under consideration by Common Council
No CIO-specific technology systems mentioned; drone program may have IT infrastructure implications
Budget vote scheduled Oct. 14; Legislature review begins Sept. 17
Lead remediation funding represents multi-year commitment ($1.23M new + $12M prior allocation)
